description: A simple and intuitive countdown timer application that allows users to set a desired duration and control the countdown with start, pause, and reset functionality. The app provides clear visual feedback of the remaining time and alerts the user when the countdown reaches zero.
features: Set custom countdown duration (hours, minutes, seconds)
Start button to begin or resume the countdown
Pause button to temporarily halt the countdown
Reset button to restore the timer to the original set duration
Visual display of remaining time in HH:MM:SS format
Audio alert or notification when countdown reaches zero
Visual alert or animation when countdown completes
Progress indicator showing elapsed vs remaining time
Preset quick-select durations (e.g., 1 min, 5 min, 10 min, 15 min, 30 min)
Disable start button when timer is already running
Disable pause button when timer is not running
Keyboard shortcut support for start, pause, and reset
Responsive design for various screen sizes
Persist timer state on accidental page refresh
Input validation to prevent negative or zero durations
Clear visual distinction between running, paused, and stopped states
Favicon or title update showing remaining time
userFlows: User sets a custom countdown duration using input fields
User selects a preset duration for quick setup
User presses start to begin the countdown
User presses pause to temporarily stop the countdown
User presses start again to resume a paused countdown
User presses reset to restore the timer to the originally set duration
User receives an audio and visual alert when the timer reaches zero
User dismisses the completion alert and optionally resets the timer
User adjusts the duration while the timer is stopped
constraints: Must be responsive and work on mobile, tablet, and desktop
Timer must remain accurate and not drift over long durations
Must provide accessible controls (ARIA labels, keyboard navigation)
UI must clearly communicate the current timer state (running, paused, idle, completed)
Should function entirely client-side with no backend required
Must handle browser tab inactivity without losing accuracy
Minimal load time with no heavy dependencies
